quizQuestion:
Les crayons {___} par douzaines.
(Pencils are sold by the dozen.)
sentenceTranslation: Pencils are sold by the dozen.
explanation: se vendent — pronominal passive of vendre. No agent is named; the subject receives the action impersonally. Third-person plural: se + vendent.

quizQuestion:
Le français {___} dans plus de 50 pays.
(French is spoken in more than 50 countries.)
sentenceTranslation: French is spoken in more than 50 countries.
explanation: se parle — pronominal passive of parler. Expresses a general truth with no specific agent. Third-person singular: se + parle.

quizQuestion:
Le carpaccio de bœuf, ça {___} cru.
(Beef carpaccio — one can eat it raw.)
sentenceTranslation: Beef carpaccio — one can eat it raw.
explanation: se mange — pronominal passive of manger. The ça se + verb construction expresses that something is done or acceptable to do, with no named agent.

quizQuestion:
Interrompre quelqu'un en pleine réunion, cela {___}.
(Interrupting someone mid-meeting — one doesn't do that.)
sentenceTranslation: Interrupting someone mid-meeting — one doesn't do that.
explanation: ne se fait pas — negative pronominal passive of faire. Expresses a social norm: the action is not acceptable. No agent named.

quizQuestion:
Ce phénomène {___} par le réchauffement climatique.
(This phenomenon is explained by climate change.)
sentenceTranslation: This phenomenon is explained by climate change.
grammarPointLabel: Passif pronominal (se) — with named agent par
explanation: s'explique — pronominal passive of expliquer. Unlike pure agent-free se-passives, this one names the agent (par le réchauffement). Both constructions are valid at B2.

quizQuestion:
Ce roman {___} facilement en un week-end.
(This novel can be read easily in one weekend.)
sentenceTranslation: This novel can be read easily in one weekend.
explanation: se lit — pronominal passive of lire. Expresses that the novel is the kind of book one reads easily; no agent named.

quizQuestion:
Ces expressions {___} surtout à l'oral.
(These expressions are mainly used in speech.)
sentenceTranslation: These expressions are mainly used in speech.
explanation: se disent — pronominal passive of dire. Plural subject ces expressions takes third-person plural: se + disent.

quizQuestion:
Cette tache {___} de loin.
(That stain can be seen from a distance.)
sentenceTranslation: That stain can be seen from a distance.
explanation: se voit — pronominal passive of voir. Singular subject la tache: se + voit. Expresses the stain is visible, with no named agent.

quizQuestion:
La musique {___} jusqu'à la rue.
(The music can be heard all the way to the street.)
sentenceTranslation: The music can be heard all the way to the street.
explanation: s'entend — pronominal passive of entendre. Singular la musique: s' + entend. No agent; expresses that the music carries that far.

quizQuestion:
Le parlement {___} suite aux élections anticipées.
(The parliament was dissolved following the snap elections.)
sentenceTranslation: The parliament has been dissolved.
grammarPointLabel: Passif — passé composé (a été + PP) masculine singular
explanation: a été dissous — passé composé passive: avoir + été + past participle of dissoudre. Subject le parlement is masculine singular; dissous agrees accordingly.

quizQuestion:
Mike {___} président lors du dernier congrès.
(Mike was elected chairperson at the last congress.)
sentenceTranslation: Mike was elected chairperson.
explanation: a été élu — passé composé passive of élire. Subject Mike is masculine singular; élu takes no extra agreement marker here.

quizQuestion:
Beaucoup d'arbres {___} pendant la tempête.
(A lot of trees were cut down during the storm.)
sentenceTranslation: A lot of trees were cut down.
grammarPointLabel: Passif — passé composé plural (ont été + PP)
explanation: ont été abattus — passé composé passive of abattre. Plural subject beaucoup d'arbres (masculine): ont été + abattus (masculine plural agreement).

quizQuestion:
Ma bicyclette {___} hier.
(My bicycle was repaired yesterday.)
sentenceTranslation: My bicycle was repaired yesterday.
grammarPointLabel: Passif — passé composé feminine (a été + PP-e)
explanation: a été réparée — passé composé passive of réparer. Subject la bicyclette is feminine singular; réparée takes the feminine -e agreement.

quizQuestion:
Le budget {___} avant la crise.
(The budget had been approved before the crisis.)
sentenceTranslation: The budget had been approved before the crisis.
explanation: avait été approuvé — plus-que-parfait passive of approuver. PQP = imparfait of avoir (avait) + été + PP. Masculine singular: approuvé.

quizQuestion:
L'accord de paix {___} la semaine prochaine.
(The peace agreement will be signed next week.)
sentenceTranslation: The peace agreement will be signed next week.
explanation: sera signé — futur simple passive of signer. Futur passif = futur of être (sera) + PP. Masculine singular: signé.

quizQuestion:
Sans la tempête, la réunion n'{___} pas.
(Without the storm, the meeting would not have been cancelled.)
sentenceTranslation: Without the storm, the meeting would not have been cancelled.
explanation: aurait été annulée — conditionnel passé passive of annuler. Conditionnel passé = conditionnel of avoir (aurait) + été + PP. Feminine singular: annulée.

quizQuestion:
Le chien {___} par toute la famille.
(The dog was raised by the whole family.)
sentenceTranslation: The dog was raised by the whole family.
grammarPointLabel: Passif — passé composé with agent par (a été + PP)
explanation: a été élevé — passé composé passive of élever. The agent par toute la famille is named explicitly. Masculine singular: élevé.

quizQuestion:
J'{___} par mon professeur pour mon retard.
(I was scolded by my teacher for being late.)
sentenceTranslation: I was scolded by my teacher for being late.
grammarPointLabel: Passif — passé composé first person (ai été + PP)
explanation: ai été grondé — passé composé passive of gronder. First-person subject je: ai été + PP. Grondé/e agrees with the speaker's gender; both forms given as acceptable.

quizQuestion:
Le pont {___} bien avant la guerre.
(The bridge had been built long before the war.)
sentenceTranslation: The bridge had been built long before the war.
explanation: avait été construit — plus-que-parfait passive of construire. PQP passif = avait + été + PP. Masculine singular: construit.

quizQuestion:
Les résultats {___} demain matin.
(The results will be published tomorrow morning.)
sentenceTranslation: The results will be published tomorrow morning.
explanation: seront publiés — futur simple passive of publier. Plural subject les résultats (masculine): seront + publiés (masculine plural agreement).

quizQuestion:
J'ai {___} ma bicyclette avant le voyage.
(I had my bicycle repaired before the trip.)
sentenceTranslation: I had my bicycle repaired before the trip.
grammarPointLabel: Faire causatif — faire + infinitif (have something done)
explanation: fait réparer — causative faire: faire + infinitive of réparer. The subject causes someone else to perform the repair; the subject does not act directly.

quizQuestion:
Le directeur a {___} tous les employés dans son bureau.
(The manager had all the employees come to his office.)
sentenceTranslation: The manager had all the employees come to his office.
grammarPointLabel: Faire causatif — faire venir (have someone come)
explanation: fait venir — causative faire + venir. The subject causes someone else to come; common in professional and formal contexts.

quizQuestion:
Ce film m'a {___} à chaudes larmes.
(That film made me cry my eyes out.)
sentenceTranslation: That film made me cry my eyes out.
grammarPointLabel: Faire causatif — faire + infinitif (cause an emotional action)
explanation: fait pleurer — causative faire + pleurer. The subject (ce film) causes the object (m') to undergo the action. Object pronoun precedes the conjugated faire.

quizQuestion:
Veuillez nous {___} votre décision avant vendredi.
(Please let us know your decision before Friday.)
sentenceTranslation: Please let us know your decision before Friday.
grammarPointLabel: Faire causatif — faire savoir (make known / inform)
explanation: faire savoir — fixed causative phrase meaning 'to make known, to inform.' Formal register; nous is the indirect object placed before the infinitive savoir.

quizQuestion:
Son attitude m'a clairement {___} que je n'étais pas le bienvenu.
(His attitude made it clearly understood that I was not welcome.)
sentenceTranslation: His attitude made it clearly understood that I was not welcome.
explanation: fait comprendre — causative faire + comprendre. Expresses that the subject caused the object to understand; the object pronoun m' precedes the conjugated verb.

quizQuestion:
La municipalité a {___} une nouvelle bibliothèque.
(The municipality had a new library built.)
sentenceTranslation: The municipality had a new library built.
explanation: fait construire — causative faire + construire. The municipality commissions the building; it does not construct it directly. No passive verb is needed when causative faire is used.

quizQuestion:
{___} ! Je peux expliquer.
(Let me speak! I can explain.)
sentenceTranslation: Let me speak! I can explain.
explanation: laissez-moi parler — laisser causative in the imperative. The subject allows the object (moi) to perform the action of speaking. Object pronoun follows the hyphen in imperative form.

quizQuestion:
Il a {___} le verre sur le sol.
(He dropped the glass on the floor.)
sentenceTranslation: He dropped the glass on the floor.
grammarPointLabel: Laisser causatif — laisser tomber (drop / let fall)
explanation: laissé tomber — laisser + tomber. Literally 'let fall'; also an idiom meaning 'to give up or abandon something.' Here used in the literal sense of letting an object fall.

quizQuestion:
Le gardien a {___} les visiteurs sans vérifier leurs badges.
(The guard let the visitors in without checking their badges.)
sentenceTranslation: The guard let the visitors in without checking their badges.
grammarPointLabel: Laisser causatif — laisser entrer (let in / allow to enter)
explanation: laissé entrer — laisser + entrer. Causative: the guard allows others to perform the action of entering. Past participle laissé is invariable before an infinitive.

quizQuestion:
Il vaut mieux {___} les experts dans ce domaine.
(It's better to let the experts handle it in this field.)
sentenceTranslation: It's better to let the experts handle it in this field.
grammarPointLabel: Laisser causatif — laisser faire (let act / allow to do)
explanation: laisser faire — laisser + faire in infinitive. Expresses non-interference; the subject allows others to act. Also the economic principle of non-intervention (laissez-faire).

quizQuestion:
Ce livre {___} à plus d'un million d'exemplaires.
(This book sells over a million copies.)
sentenceTranslation: This book sells over a million copies.
explanation: se vend — pronominal passive of vendre. Singular subject ce livre: se + vend. Expresses a general commercial truth without naming an agent.

quizQuestion:
Il nous a {___} pendant deux heures sans explication.
(He kept us waiting for two hours without explanation.)
sentenceTranslation: He kept us waiting for two hours without explanation.
grammarPointLabel: Faire causatif — faire attendre (keep someone waiting)
explanation: fait attendre — causative faire + attendre. Expresses that the subject caused the object (nous) to wait. Object pronoun nous precedes the conjugated a fait.
